name: Fluted pilaster
description: Flat decorative column that projects one block outward from the face of a wall, evoking the load-bearing shaft of a classical order without actually carrying weight. A pilaster comprises three stacked elements pressed against the wall surface: a single-block base course at floor level, a slender vertical shaft four to seven blocks tall, and a crowning capital simplified from the Doric or Ionic orders (a flat slab, optionally flared with outward-facing stairs). In voxel form, fluting is implied rather than modeled — the smooth white face of quartz or the warm grain of sandstone reads as a fluted shaft at building scale. The architecture_planner uses pilasters to articulate Renaissance and neoclassical facades, frame doorways, and rhythmize the bays of monumental interiors, providing a levels-of-scale break between plain wall and ornamented entablature.
